summary: Fixture manifest for the catalogctl Azure-executability verify lane — mirrors two REAL azure-compute.json set_attribute op SHAPES so the golden harness proves them byte-exact: a scalar target.attr resize (azurerm_linux_virtual_machine.size) and a grow-only numeric bound (azurerm_managed_disk.disk_size_gb, bounds.growOnly). The op shapes (codemodOp/target/params/bounds) mirror the app catalog; only the prose is trimmed.

operations.title: Resize a Linux virtual machine
operations.description: Change the size argument (the Azure VM SKU) to an allowlisted value. Applied in place; Azure deallocates and restarts the VM to move it onto the new size.

params.name: virtual_machine
params.label: Virtual machine
params.help: Select the virtual machine to resize; the picker only lists VMs already tracked in inventory.

params.name: size
params.label: New size
params.help: The Azure VM size (SKU) that fixes the host's vCPU and memory.

operations.title: Grow a managed disk
operations.description: Increase a managed disk's size via the disk_size_gb argument. Grow-only — an Azure managed disk cannot be shrunk, so this is irreversible.

params.name: managed_disk
params.label: Managed disk
params.help: Select the managed disk to enlarge; the size field separately enforces grow-only.

params.name: disk_size_gb
params.label: New size (GiB)
params.help: Must exceed the current size in GiB; a managed disk cannot be shrunk.
